texto: I have lived so long among people who do not understand me, been so long accustomed to refrain and disguise myself for fear of being laughed at, that I have grown as difficult to come at as a snail in a shell; and what is worse, I cannot come out of my shell when I wish it.
explicacao: Citação de Jane Welsh Carlyle sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I have lived so long among people who do not understand me, been so long accustomed to refrain an…” articula humor, myself, me em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e me.

texto: The only thing that makes one place more attractive to me than another is the quantity of heart I find in it.
explicacao: Citação de Jane Welsh Carlyle sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The only thing that makes one place more attractive to me than another is the quantity of heart I…” articula reflexao, side, thing em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Blessed be the inventor of photography! I set him above even the inventor of chloroform! It has given more positive pleasure to po or suffering humanity than anything else that has "cast up" in my time or is like to--this art by which even the "poor" can possess themselves of tolerable likenesses of their absent dear ones. And mustn't it be acting favourably on the morality of the country?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jane Welsh Carlyle: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Blessed be the inventor of photography! I set him above even the inventor of chloroform! It has g…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, beauty, comfort pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de comfort e photography.
